data.text: I’ve confirmed the loop still owns the decomposition and push-failure parking branches. Next I’m reading the attempt-layer tests and the structured outcome types so I can move the behavior without changing event payloads or cooldown semantics.

One of the file reads failed because I fat-fingered the worktree path in the command. I’m correcting that now and checking the surrounding execute-loop logic that already consumes `attemptOut.Parking`, since the safest change is to extend that path instead of inventing a second parking mechanism.
The worktree path was missing the separator in my command. I’m rerunning the reads against the exact execution directory so I can patch the smallest possible surface.

data.output:
1	package agent
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"strings"
     6		"testing"
     7		"time"
     8	
     9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
    11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    13	)
    14	
    15	// TestClassifyExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed pins the contract that a merged
    16	// outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker classifies as
    17	// push_failed (not success), so the execute-loop refuses to close the bead.
    18	func TestClassifyExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed(t *testing.T) {
    19		got := ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("merged", 0, PushFailedReasonPrefix+" remote rejected: pre-receive hook declined")
    20		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed, got,
    21			"merged outcome with push-failed reason must classify as push_failed, not success")
    22	
    23		gotMerged := ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("merged", 0, "merged onto current tip")
    24		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, gotMerged,
    25			"merged outcome without push-failed reason must still classify as success")
    26	}
    27	
    28	func TestClassifyExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview(t *testing.T) {
    29		for _, reason := range []string{
    30			"large-deletion gate: huge.txt deleted 250 lines (threshold 200)",
    31			"syntax sanity gate: config.json: invalid JSON",
    32			"post-land gate failed: make test: exit status 2",
    33		} {
    34			got := ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("preserved", 0, reason)
    35			assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, got, reason)
    36		}
    37	}
    38	
    39	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedStaysOpenAndParks asserts the AC for
    40	// ddx-af54ebf3:
    41	//   - bead is NOT closed (status remains open)
    42	//   - last_status=push_failed and the push stderr are stored as cooldown metadata
    43	//   - the bead's events surface the push stderr so an operator can see it
    44	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedStaysOpenAndParks(t *testing.T) {
    45		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
    46		pushStderr := "remote: error: GH001: large files detected; aborting"
    47		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
    48			Store: store,
    49			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
    50				return ExecuteBeadReport{
    51					BeadID:    beadID,
    52					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed,
    53					Detail:    PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + pushStderr,
    54					SessionID: "sess-push-failed",
    55					BaseRev:   "aaaa1111",
    56					ResultRev: "bbbb2222",
    57				}, nil
    58			}),
    59		}
    60	
    61		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
    62		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
    63		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
    64		require.NoError(t, err)
    65		require.NotNil(t, result)
    66		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, result.Failures, 1)
    67		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed, result.LastFailureStatus)
    68	
    69		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
    70		require.NoError(t, err)
    71		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "push_failed must NOT close the bead")
    72		assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "push_failed must release the claim")
    73	
    74		require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
    75		assert.Equal(t, "push_failed", got.Extra["execute-loop-last-status"],
    76			"loop must record last_status=push_failed so subsequent claim attempts can refuse")
    77		detail, _ := got.Extra["execute-loop-last-detail"].(string)
    78		assert.Contains(t, detail, pushStderr,
    79			"loop must record the push stderr in last_detail so operators can see why")
    80		assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["execute-loop-retry-after"],
    81			"push_failed must park the bead via execute-loop-retry-after")
    82	
    83		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
    84		require.NoError(t, err)
    85		require.NotEmpty(t, events)
    86		var sawPushStderr bool
    87		for _, ev := range events {
    88			if ev.Summary == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed && strings.Contains(ev.Body, pushStderr) {
    89				sawPushStderr = true
    90				break
    91			}
    92		}
    93		assert.True(t, sawPushStderr,
    94			"push stderr must appear in a bead event so operators can see why the push failed")
    95	}
    96	
    97	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedCooldownCappedAt24h pins AC #2 + #7 of
    98	// ddx-a458af7c: push_failed (and every other loop-set cooldown) caps at
    99	// MaxLoopCooldown (24h). Year-scale parks are an operator decision via
   100	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, never an automatic
   101	// loop output.
   102	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushFailedCooldownCappedAt24h(t *testing.T) {
   103		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
   104		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
   105			Store: store,
   106			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
   107				return ExecuteBeadReport{
   108					BeadID:    beadID,
   109					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed,
   110					Detail:    PushFailedReasonPrefix + " remote rejected",
   111					SessionID: "sess-cap",
   112					BaseRev:   "aaaa",
   113					ResultRev: "bbbb",
   114				}, nil
   115			}),
   116		}
   117	
   118		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
   119		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
   120		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
   121		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
   122		require.NoError(t, err)
   123	
   124		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
   125		require.NoError(t, err)
   126		retryAfter, _ := got.Extra["execute-loop-retry-after"].(string)
   127		require.NotEmpty(t, retryAfter, "push_failed must park the bead")
   128		parsed, perr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, retryAfter)
   129		require.NoError(t, perr, "execute-loop-retry-after must parse as RFC3339")
   130		delta := parsed.Sub(startedAt)
   131		assert.LessOrEqual(t, delta, MaxLoopCooldown+time.Minute,
   132			"push_failed cooldown must cap at MaxLoopCooldown (24h), got %s", delta)
   133	}
   134	
   135	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushConflictParksAndEmitsEvent pins AC #3 + #5 of
   136	// ddx-a458af7c: push_conflict (auto-merge during push recovery failed)
   137	// emits a structured `push-conflict` event, parks the bead under the 24h
   138	// cap, and is distinct from generic execution_failed.
   139	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerPushConflictParksAndEmitsEvent(t *testing.T) {
   140		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
   141		conflictDetail := PushConflictReasonPrefix + " auto-recovery retry push: CONFLICT (content)"
   142		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
   143			Store: store,
   144			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
   145				return ExecuteBeadReport{
   146					BeadID:    beadID,
   147					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict,
   148					Detail:    conflictDetail,
   149					SessionID: "sess-pconf",
   150					BaseRev:   "aaaa1111",
   151					ResultRev: "cccc3333",
   152				}, nil
   153			}),
   154		}
   155	
   156		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
   157		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
   158		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
   159		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
   160		require.NoError(t, err)
   161		require.NotNil(t, result)
   162		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict, result.LastFailureStatus)
   163	
   164		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
   165		require.NoError(t, err)
   166		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "push_conflict must NOT close the bead")
   167		require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
   168		assert.Equal(t, "push_conflict", got.Extra["execute-loop-last-status"])
   169	
   170		// Cooldown capped at 24h.
   171		retryAfter, _ := got.Extra["execute-loop-retry-after"].(string)
   172		require.NotEmpty(t, retryAfter)
   173		parsed, perr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, retryAfter)
   174		require.NoError(t, perr)
   175		delta := parsed.Sub(startedAt)
   176		assert.LessOrEqual(t, delta, MaxLoopCooldown+time.Minute,
   177			"push_conflict cooldown must cap at MaxLoopCooldown (24h), got %s", delta)
   178	
   179		// Structured push-conflict event with the conflict context.
   180		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
   181		require.NoError(t, err)
   182		var sawPushConflict bool
   183		for _, ev := range events {
   184			if ev.Kind == "push-conflict" {
   185				sawPushConflict = true
   186				assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, conflictDetail,
   187					"push-conflict event body must record the conflict detail")
   188				break
   189			}
   190		}
   191		assert.True(t, sawPushConflict,
   192			"loop must emit a kind:push-conflict event so operators can see the conflict context")
   193	}
   194	
   195	// TestClaimRefusesPushFailedBead pins the AC that subsequent claim attempts
   196	// on a push-failed bead fail loudly until the operator clears
   197	// execute-loop-last-status.
   198	func TestClaimRefusesPushFailedBead(t *testing.T) {
   199		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
   200		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
   201	
   202		b := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-pf01", Title: "push-failed", Priority: 0}
   203		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
   204	
   205		require.NoError(t, store.Update(b.ID, func(bb *bead.Bead) {
   206			if bb.Extra == nil {
   207				bb.Extra = map[string]any{}
   208			}
   209			bb.Extra["execute-loop-last-status"] = "push_failed"
   210			bb.Extra["execute-loop-last-detail"] = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " remote: GH001: large files detected"
   211		}))
   212	
   213		err := store.Claim(b.ID, "worker")
   214		require.Error(t, err, "Claim must refuse a bead whose last_status=push_failed")
   215		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "push failed")
   216		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "GH001",
   217			"the previous push stderr must surface in the claim error so it fails loudly")
   218	
   219		// Clearing the last_status restores claimability.
   220		require.NoError(t, store.Update(b.ID, func(bb *bead.Bead) {
   221			delete(bb.Extra, "execute-loop-last-status")
   222			delete(bb.Extra, "execute-loop-last-detail")
   223		}))
   224		require.NoError(t, store.Claim(b.ID, "worker"),
   225			"Claim must succeed after operator clears execute-loop-last-status")
   226	}

1	package try
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"encoding/json"
     6		"fmt"
     7		"time"
     8	
     9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
    10	)
    11	
    12	const (
    13		StatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
    14		StatusLandConflictNeedsHuman   = "land_conflict_needs_human"
    15	)
    16	
    17	type ConflictRecoveryDisposition int
    18	
    19	const (
    20		ConflictRecoveryMerged ConflictRecoveryDisposition = iota
    21		ConflictRecoveryPark
    22		ConflictRecoveryNeedsHuman
    23	)
    24	
    25	type ConflictRecoveryInput struct {
    26		Bead             bead.Bead
    27		Report           Report
    28		ProjectRoot      string
    29		AutoRecover      ConflictAutoRecoverFn
    30		ConflictResolver ConflictResolverFn
    31		Store            Store
    32		Assignee         string
    33		Now              func() time.Time
    34		Cooldown         time.Duration
    35	}
    36	
    37	type ConflictRecoveryOutput struct {
    38		Report      Report
    39		Disposition ConflictRecoveryDisposition
    40		StoreErrOp  string
    41		StoreErr    error
    42	}
    43	
    44	func ConflictRecoveryOutcome(ctx context.Context, in ConflictRecoveryInput) Outcome {
    45		out := RunConflictRecovery(ctx, in)
    46		disposition := OutcomePark
    47		if out.Disposition == ConflictRecoveryMerged {
    48			disposition = OutcomeSuccess
    49		}
    50		return Outcome{
    51			Report:      out.Report,
    52			Disposition: disposition,
    53			StoreErrOp:  out.StoreErrOp,
    54			StoreErr:    out.StoreErr,
    55		}
    56	}
    57	
    58	func RunConflictRecovery(ctx context.Context, in ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput {
    59		report := in.Report
    60		out := ConflictRecoveryOutput{Report: report}
    61	
    62		now := in.Now
    63		if now == nil {
    64			now = time.Now
    65		}
    66	
    67		autoFn := in.AutoRecover
    68		if autoFn == nil {
    69			autoFn = func(string, string) (string, error) {
    70				return "", fmt.Errorf("conflict auto-recover function is required")
    71			}
    72		}
    73	
    74		newTip, autoErr := autoFn(in.ProjectRoot, report.PreserveRef)
    75		if autoErr == nil && newTip != "" {
    76			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
    77				Kind:      "land-conflict-auto-recovered",
    78				Summary:   "preserved iteration auto-recovered onto current tip via ort",
    79				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s\nnew_tip=%s", report.PreserveRef, newTip),
    80				Actor:     in.Assignee,
    81				Source:    "ddx agent try",
    82				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
    83			})
    84			report.Status = StatusSuccess
    85			report.ResultRev = newTip
    86			report.Detail = "auto-recovered: merged preserved iteration onto current tip via ort"
    87			if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
    88				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
    89				out.StoreErr = err
    90				out.Report = report
    91				return out
    92			}
    93			out.Report = report
    94			out.Disposition = ConflictRecoveryMerged
    95			return out
    96		}
    97	
    98		if in.ConflictResolver != nil {
    99			resolvedTip, isBlocking, resolveErr := in.ConflictResolver(ctx, in.Bead.ID, report.PreserveRef, in.ProjectRoot)
   100			if resolveErr == nil && resolvedTip != "" {
   101				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
   102					Kind:      "land-conflict-auto-recovered",
   103					Summary:   "preserved iteration resolved by focused conflict-resolve agent",
   104					Body:      fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s\nnew_tip=%s", report.PreserveRef, resolvedTip),
   105					Actor:     in.Assignee,
   106					Source:    "ddx agent try",
   107					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
   108				})
   109				report.Status = StatusSuccess
   110				report.ResultRev = resolvedTip
   111				report.Detail = "auto-recovered: focused conflict-resolve agent landed preserved iteration"
   112				if err := in.Store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
   113					out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
   114					out.StoreErr = err
   115					out.Report = report
   116					return out
   117				}
   118				out.Report = report
   119				out.Disposition = ConflictRecoveryMerged
   120				return out
   121			}
   122			if isBlocking {
   123				report.Status = StatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
   124			} else {
   125				report.Status = StatusLandConflictUnresolvable
   126			}
   127		} else {
   128			report.Status = StatusLandConflictUnresolvable
   129		}
   130	
   131		autoErrMsg := ""
   132		if autoErr != nil {
   133			autoErrMsg = autoErr.Error()
   134		}
   135		body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
   136			"preserve_ref":     report.PreserveRef,
   137			"base_rev":         report.BaseRev,
   138			"result_rev":       report.ResultRev,
   139			"session_id":       report.SessionID,
   140			"auto_merge_error": autoErrMsg,
   141		})
   142		bodyStr := report.PreserveRef
   143		if mErr == nil {
   144			bodyStr = string(body)
   145		}
   146		eventKind := "land-conflict-unresolvable"
   147		if report.Status == StatusLandConflictNeedsHuman {
   148			eventKind = "land-conflict-needs-human"
   149		}
   150		_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
   151			Kind:      eventKind,
   152			Summary:   "preserved iteration could not be auto-recovered; parked for operator",
   153			Body:      bodyStr,
   154			Actor:     in.Assignee,
   155			Source:    "ddx agent try",
   156			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
   157		})
   158		report.Detail = report.Status + ": preserve_ref=" + report.PreserveRef
   159	
   160		if err := in.Store.Unclaim(in.Bead.ID); err != nil {
   161			out.StoreErrOp = "Unclaim"
   162			out.StoreErr = err
   163			out.Report = report
   164			return out
   165		}
   166		parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(in.Cooldown)
   167		if err := in.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(in.Bead.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
   168			out.StoreErrOp = "SetExecutionCooldown"
   169			out.StoreErr = err
   170			out.Report = report
   171			return out
   172		}
   173		report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
   174	
   175		out.Report = report
   176		if report.Status == StatusLandConflictNeedsHuman {
   177			out.Disposition = ConflictRecoveryNeedsHuman
   178		} else {
   179			out.Disposition = ConflictRecoveryPark
   180		}
   181		return out
   182	}

   190	
   191	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
   192	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
   193	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
   194	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
   195	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
   196		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
   197		if hook == nil {
   198			return report
   199		}
   200		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
   201		if err != nil {
   202			if runtime.Log != nil {
   203				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
   204			}
   205			return report
   206		}
   207		if strings.TrimSpace(triage.Classification) == "" {
   208			return report
   209		}
   210		report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
   211		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
   212		return report
   213	}
   214	
   215	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
   216	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
   217	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
   218	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
   219		if d <= 0 {
   220			return 0
   221		}
   222		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
   223			return MaxLoopCooldown
   224		}
   225		return d
   226	}
   227	
   228	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
   229		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
   230		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
   231		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
   232		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
   233		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
   234		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
   235		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
   236		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
   237		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
   238		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
   239		ActualPower                 int           `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
   240		PredictedPower              int           `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
   241		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64       `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
   242		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64       `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
   243		PredictedCostSource         string        `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
   244		Status                      string        `json:"status"`
   245		Detail                      string        `json:"detail,omitempty"`
   246		Error                       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
   247		Stderr                      string        `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
   248		RateLimitBudget             time.Duration `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
   249		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
   250		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
   251		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
   252		PreserveRef                 string        `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
   253		RetryAfter                  string        `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
   254		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
   255		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
   256		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
   257		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
   258		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
   259		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
   260		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
   261		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
   262		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
   263		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
   264		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
   265		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
   266		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
   267		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
   268		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
   269		// compute wasted/effective spend.
   270		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
   271		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
   272		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
   273		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
   274		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
   275		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
   276		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
   277		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
   278		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
   279		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
   280		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
   281		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
   282		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
   283		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
   284		// recommendation.
   285		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
   286		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
   287		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
   288		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
   289		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
   290		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
   291		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
   292		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
   293		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
   294		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
   295		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
   296		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
   297		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
   298		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
   299		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
   300		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
   301		// occurring.
   302		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
   303		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
   304		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
   305		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
   306		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
   307	}

   261	
   262	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
   263	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
   264	const (
   265		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
   266		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
   267		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
   268		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
   269		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
   270		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
   271		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
   272		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
   273		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
   274		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
   275		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
   276		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
   277	
   278		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
   279		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
   280		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
   281		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
   282		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
   283		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
   284		//
   285		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
   286		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
   287		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
   288		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
   289		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
   290		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
   291	
   292		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
   293		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
   294		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
   295		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
   296		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
   297	
   298		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
   299		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
   300		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
   301		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
   302		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
   303	
   304		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman is set when the focused
   305		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
   306		// human judgment, not automated retry. Parked under the same short
   307		// cooldown with a kind:land-conflict-needs-human event.
   308		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman = "land_conflict_needs_human"
   309	)
   310	
   311	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
   312	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
   313	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
   314	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
   315		if exitCode != 0 {
   316			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
   317		}
   318	
   319		switch outcome {
   320		case "merged":
   321			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
   322			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
   323			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
   324			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
   325			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
   326			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
   327				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
   328			}
   329			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
   330				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
   331			}
   332			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
   333		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
   334			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
   335		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
   336			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
   337		case "error":
   338			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
   339		case "preserved":
   340			switch reason {
